chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q1_analysis: The paper explicitly provides a working link to the full prototype implementation in Section I (Contributions): https://github.com/vusec/firestarter.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q2_analysis: The evaluation relies entirely on software simulations using standard web servers (Nginx, Apache, Lighttpd) running on an Intel i7-6700K machine. It uses fault injection frameworks (HSFI) and benchmarks but does not mention physical hardware testbeds or massive-scale cloud deployments.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q3_analysis: Section II ('FAULT MODEL') contains a clear, explicit definition of the fault model, specifying 'fail-stop' for crashes caused by both transient and persistent faults, and detailing assumptions about memory corruption bugs detected by compilers.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q4_analysis: The paper provides rigorous data-driven analysis of performance trade-offs in Section VI-D. It quantifies runtime overhead (e.g., 17% on Nginx), analyzes the impact of adaptive transaction policies on HTM abort rates, and compares overhead against STM-only and HTM-only baselines.

major_weakness: The experimental evaluation is limited to software simulations on a single machine without physical hardware testbeds or large-scale cloud deployments.
